response.justification: Candidate is a strong full-stack software developer with web, mobile, and some embedded/C/C++ exposure, but the role requires a Principal-level Performance Architect specializing in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware optimization, and deep neural network workload analysis. The resume shows no experience with hardware-software co-design, GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture, deep learning training/inference optimization, or system-on-chip design. Educational background (BS in CS) without specialized hardware/architecture credentials further misaligns with the role.
strengths: Long tenure and breadth across multiple languages including C/C++ at National Instruments
Demonstrated low-latency systems work at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests/second
Strong engineering fundamentals: TDD, CI/CD, and developer tooling experience
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, or AI accelerator experience
No background in deep learning workload optimization, GPU, SIMD, or MIMD architectures
Profile is full-stack web/app development, not hardware performance architecture at Principal level

key_factors.factor: Missing AI accelerator / SoC modeling experience
No GPU / SIMD / MIMD / DNN optimization background
Some low-level C/C++ and low-latency systems exposure
